query: OpenSEO complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: OpenSEO - Thank you for the support! Also, I added Backlink Analysis...
results.content:
- This looks sick!! Any plans on adding local SEO support? Heat maps, citations, etc.
- Is this a self-hosted analyzer for your SEO or i'm missing something? Not versed in this kind of topic, sorry if i sound dumb
- Why should I use your tool when SerpBear (  ) already exists? That would also make good content for a comparison page serpbear vs openseo.
- We'll probably still use some of the other features, but if I'm understanding this correctly the backlinks feature depends on DataForSEO. Which basically means you have to throw $100 every single damn month at their API just to use it. That’s honestly pretty frustrating if you're just trying to self-host and experiment with the tool. A $100/month barrier just to try the backlinks feature feels pretty ridiculous. [...] - Congrats on 500 stars — the Docker improvement was probably the right call,thta kind of friction kills adoption fast. The LLM mention API integration is the part I'm most interested in. Most tools in this space track rankings and backlinks but measuring AI citation rates is still largely manual — run queries, log results, repeat. If OpenSEO can pull LLM mention data reliably at scale that's a genuinely different capability from anything else in the open source space right now. A few things that would make that feature particularly useful in practice: Query set management — being able to define a fixed set of queries and track citation rate over time rather than spot-checking. The signal only becomes meaningful when you can measure change from a baseline. Engine breakdown — Perplexity, [...] A couple weeks ago I posted my project, OpenSEO , and was overwhelmed by the support it got from this community. It just passed 500 stars on Github and I think its the second most upvoted post in this subreddit which is crazy to me. When I originally posted, there were lots of rough edges that I think were preventing people from actually trying it out. These last few weeks I've been making lots of improvements to make it really easy to get started with Docker + improving the documentation. The top feature requests have been 1. Backlinks 2. SERP Rank Tracking. I just pushed a new release adding support for backlinks. Next, I'll tackle Rank Tracking. Let me know if you have any specific workflows or gripes with other products that I should consider. This is probably the last product-update

results.title: OpenSEO Has 1.7k GitHub Stars. I Built the Same Thing for ...
results.content:
This is the same principle behind the Cloudflare-based automations I build for clients — edge-deployed, real output, not cached assumptions.

That's not a criticism of OpenSEO. It's a different architectural choice with a real tradeoff.

## What OpenSEO has that I don't

I'll be honest:

If you need those features and you're comfortable with the DataForSEO cost model, OpenSEO is a reasonable choice.

But if your core need is: does this page have what Google needs to rank it — a real browser costs less and sees more.

## One more thing

OpenSEO's contributor list includes @claude.

So does mine.

We're both building production tools with the Claude API. The difference is what you're optimizing for — features, or cost per insight.

I chose cost per insight. My sites are proof it works. [...] lucky3mc profile image

Great breakdown, Dan. The real-browser vs API-index distinction is the kind of architectural thinking most SEO tools skip. I actually ran a 9-engine security scan on your repo out of curiosity came back clean. Zero findings. For a tool that runs Playwright against arbitrary URLs and processes content through LLM APIs, that's not trivial. Most AI-assisted repos I've scanned have at least a few dependency CVEs or config issues. Clean code, open source, and real browser rendering. That's a solid combination.  
Starred. Looking forward to seeing where you take this.

dannwaneri profile image
Subscriber

results.title: every-app/open-seo: Open source alternative to Semrush and Ahrefs
results.content:
## Cloudflare Self-Hosting

### Deploy the Worker

Clicking this button opens a page to deploy OpenSEO in your Cloudflare account. If you do not have an account yet, it will take you to account creation first (OpenSEO works great on the free plan).

Reference these docs while deploying since the Cloudflare UI doesn't indicate what steps you need to take: `docs/SELF_HOSTING_CLOUDFLARE.md`.

## Local Development

See `docs/LOCAL_DEVELOPMENT.md`.

Contributions are very welcome.

 Open an issue for bugs, UX friction, or feature requests.
 Open a PR if you want to implement a feature directly.
 Community-driven improvements are prioritized, and high-quality PRs are encouraged.

If you want to contribute but are unsure where to start, open an issue and describe what you want to build. [...] Open source alternative to Semrush and Ahrefs
